landing.hero_description: A science-based 30-day program to rewire your habits, manage emotions, and build a stronger mind.
landing.features_tcc_desc: Strategies validated by cognitive psychology for real results.
landing.features_guide_desc: You are not alone. Each day includes a lesson and a small practical challenge.
landing.features_offline_desc: Take your progress with you. Designed to work wherever you are.
landing.pillar_thoughts_desc: Identify and challenge negative patterns limiting your potential.
landing.pillar_activities_desc: Reconnect with pleasure and movement through daily actions.
landing.pillar_relationships_desc: Strengthen your bonds and learn to set healthy boundaries.
landing.steps_subtitle: A process optimized to avoid overwhelm and maximize habit formation.
landing.step_1_desc: Dedicate 10-15 minutes daily to reflections and proposed exercises.
landing.step_2_desc: Complete small challenges designed to shift your perspective day by day.
landing.step_3_desc: Watch your well-being levels change with our tracking journal.
landing.download_desc: Download our Android app to track your progress without distractions, with daily notifications and immediate access.
landing.faq_a1: No. It is a one-time payment that gives you lifetime access to all challenge content and future updates.
landing.faq_a2: Not at all. Mindful30 is designed to guide you from scratch with clear language and practical exercises.
landing.faq_q3: Does it work on iOS or iPhone?
landing.faq_a3: Yes! Mindful30 is a PWA (Progressive Web App). On your iPhone, simply tap the 'Share' button in Safari and select 'Add to Home Screen'. You'll have the app installed without going through the App Store.
landing.faq_a4: Absolutely. Your reflections and journal are saved locally on your device. We do not have access to your thoughts.
landing.footer_description: Empowering people to reclaim their peace of mind through science and daily action.
landing.footer_disclaimer: Note: Mindful30 is a support tool and does not substitute professional medical or psychological treatment.
landing.security_desc: When installing an app from outside the Google Play Store, Android displays security warnings. This is normal and safe with Mindful30.
landing.security_warning_title: Why do I see a warning?
landing.security_warning_desc: Google Play Protect alerts about any directly downloaded app (APK) that is not yet in its main database. Our app is 100% safe and does not collect personal data.
landing.security_step_1_desc: Press the download button and confirm you want to download the file.
landing.security_step_2_desc: Once downloaded, open the file. If the system asks for permission to 'Install from this source', grant it.
landing.security_step_3_desc: If 'App blocked' appears, click 'More details' and then 'Install anyway'.

paywall.description_demo: To access the Mindful30 Challenge and start your transformation, you need to unlock the full content.
paywall.description_premium: This section requires the Full Challenge. Unlock all 30 days to continue your transformation.
paywall.redeem_text: Already bought the challenge? Redeem your code here:
paywall.unlock_desc: You have completed your free days. Join thousands of people who are transforming their lives with the full program.
paywall.feature_2: More than 90 interactive resources and scripts
paywall.have_code: Do you have a promo code or web license?

completion.title: Challenge Completed!
completion.message: You have dedicated 30 days to your mental well-being. This is not the end, but the beginning of a new way of seeing life. Keep everything you've learned.
completion.feedback_desc: How was your experience? Help us improve Mindful30 for future versions.

dashboard.welcome_message: Remember to complete your tasks today to keep building your mental resilience.
dashboard.reset_confirm: Are you sure you want to restart your progress? This will reset your start date to today.
dashboard.locked_message: This content will unlock as the real challenge progresses. Come back tomorrow to continue.
dashboard.demo_complete_alert: Congratulations on completing the Demo! The rest of the way awaits you in the full version.
dashboard.next_day_alert: Good job. Day {{next}} will unlock tomorrow.

thoughts_page.title: Thoughts
thoughts_page.subtitle: Your exercises and reflections.
thoughts_page.empty_locked: You haven't unlocked thought tasks yet. Come back tomorrow!

activities_page.title: Activities
activities_page.subtitle: Movement and well-being.
activities_page.empty_locked: Start the challenge to unlock physical and mental activities.

relationships_page.title: Relationships
relationships_page.subtitle: Bonds and connections.
relationships_page.empty_locked: Connection tools will unlock over time. Keep going!

quotes.1: The first step doesn't take you where you want to go, but it takes you out of where you are.
quotes.2: Your mind is a garden. Your thoughts are the seeds. You can grow flowers or you can grow weeds.
quotes.3: You don't have to control your thoughts. You just have to stop letting them control you.
quotes.4: Resilience is seeing there is light despite all of the darkness.
quotes.5: Happiness is not something you postpone for the future; it is something you design for the present.
quotes.6: What you deny submits you. What you accept transforms you.
quotes.7: Sometimes the bravest thing you can do is keep living when you want to give up.
quotes.8: Your emotions are messengers, not masters. Listen to them, but you direct the way.
quotes.9: Inner peace begins the moment you choose not to allow another person or event to control your emotions.
quotes.10: You are not your thoughts; you are the observer of your thoughts.
quotes.11: Discipline is choosing between what you want now and what you want most.
quotes.12: Each day is a new opportunity to be the best version of yourself.
quotes.13: Failure is only the opportunity to begin again, this time more intelligently.
quotes.14: Caring for yourself is not selfishness, it is survival.
quotes.15: You're halfway there. Don't look at what's missing, look at how far you've come.
quotes.16: The mind is like a parachute: it only works if it's open.
quotes.17: Don't count the days, make the days count.
quotes.18: The greatest victory is the victory over oneself.
quotes.19: Breathe. It's just a bad day, not a bad life.
quotes.20: Gratitude turns what we have into enough.
quotes.21: Perfectionism is the enemy of progress.
quotes.22: You are the captain of your soul and the owner of your destiny.
quotes.23: Even the largest tree began as a small seed that stayed in its place.
quotes.24: Don't compare yourself to others, compare yourself to who you were yesterday.
quotes.25: Patience is bitter, but its fruit is sweet.
quotes.26: Be kind to yourself. You are doing the best you can.
quotes.27: Change is hard at first, messy in the middle, and beautiful at the end.
quotes.28: Your potential is infinite. The only limit is the one you place on yourself.
quotes.29: You've almost got it. Consistency is the bridge between your goals and your achievements.
quotes.30: Today we celebrate your commitment. Well-being is not a goal, it's a lifestyle.

resources.locked_message: This challenge will unlock on day {{day}}. Keep practicing today's tasks.
resources.not_found_message: Sorry, the resource you are looking for does not exist.

laughter.title: Laughter Therapy
laughter.message: Laughter releases endorphins instantly. If you don't have a video handy, try smiling exaggeratedly for 1 minute. Your brain won't notice the difference!

steps.title: 1. Shoulder Rolls
steps.desc: Roll your shoulders up, back, and down in slow circles. Repeat 5 times.

steps.title: 2. Cat Stretch
steps.desc: If seated, arch your back and then stretch your chest forward. Repeat 3 times.

steps.title: 3. Tension Shake
steps.desc: Shake your hands and feet vigorously for 10 seconds to release stress.

thoughts.title: Catching Thoughts
thoughts.description: The first step to change is realizing what passes through our mind.
thoughts.actionItem: Write down 3 automatic negative thoughts you have today.

activities.title: Small Pleasures
activities.description: Reconnecting with activities we enjoy improves mood.
activities.actionItem: Dedicate 15 minutes to an activity you enjoy (reading, music, walking).

relationships.title: Conscious Connection
relationships.description: Our relationships are fundamental to well-being.
relationships.actionItem: Send a gratitude message to someone important to you.

thoughts.title: Reality vs. Thought
thoughts.description: Learn to distinguish between what happens and what your mind interprets.
thoughts.actionItem: Choose a thought from yesterday and look for 2 pieces of objective evidence that it's true and 2 that it's not.

activities.title: Gentle Movement
activities.description: Light exercise releases endorphins and reduces cortisol.
activities.actionItem: Perform 10 minutes of stretching or gentle yoga upon waking or before bed.

relationships.title: Active Listening
relationships.description: Listening deeply strengthens bonds.
relationships.actionItem: In your next conversation, listen without interrupting and summarize what the other person said before responding.

thoughts.title: The Mental Filter
thoughts.description: Sometimes we only see the negative, ignoring the positive that happens.
thoughts.actionItem: Write down 3 good things that happened today, no matter how small they seem.

activities.title: Digital Detox
activities.description: Digital overstimulation exhausts our mental resources.
activities.actionItem: Spend a full hour without checking your phone or social media.

relationships.title: Saying No
relationships.description: Setting boundaries is a form of self-care.
relationships.actionItem: Identify a request you would want to say "no" and practice the assertiveness script.

thoughts.title: Catastrophizing
thoughts.description: Our mind sometimes jumps ahead to the worst possible scenario.
thoughts.actionItem: If you're worried about something, ask yourself: "What's the worst that could happen? How likely is it?"

activities.title: Mindful Nutrition
activities.description: What we eat influences how we feel.
activities.actionItem: Have one meal today without distractions (TV, phone), focusing only on the flavors.

relationships.title: Asking for Help
relationships.description: Recognizing that we need others makes us human and brings us together.
relationships.actionItem: Ask for help today for something small, even if you could do it alone.

thoughts.title: Negative Labels
thoughts.description: The labels we put on ourselves are often unfair and limit us.
thoughts.actionItem: Change an "I'm a disaster" to "I committed an error in this specific task."

activities.title: Tidy Environment
activities.description: External order helps promote internal order.
activities.actionItem: Organize a drawer or corner for 10 minutes.

relationships.title: Sincere Compliment
relationships.description: Recognizing the good in others generates a positive spiral.
relationships.actionItem: Give a sincere compliment to a co-worker or friend.

thoughts.title: Emotional Reasoning
thoughts.description: "If I feel bad, it's because something is wrong". Feeling is not reasoning.
thoughts.actionItem: Identify a moment today where your emotions guided your judgment and try to see the facts.

activities.title: Free Creativity
activities.description: Expressing ourselves creatively is an escape route for stress.
activities.actionItem: Draw, write, or cook something new today, without looking for perfection.

relationships.title: Quality Time
relationships.description: What matters is not the time, but the presence.
relationships.actionItem: Call a family member or close friend and dedicate 15 minutes of full presence.

thoughts.title: Weekly Review
thoughts.description: Observe your thought patterns from this week.
thoughts.actionItem: Which distortion has been the most frequent in your thought records?

activities.title: Relaxing Bath
activities.description: Warm water helps relax accumulated muscle tension.
activities.actionItem: Take a mindful shower or bath, paying attention to the sensation of the water.

relationships.title: Inner Circle
relationships.description: Strengthen your support network.
relationships.actionItem: Write down who are the 3 people you trust most and why.

thoughts.title: Personalization
thoughts.description: Not everything that happens around you is your fault or about you.
thoughts.actionItem: If someone is in a bad mood, look for 3 external reasons that have nothing to do with you.

activities.title: New Route
activities.description: Novelty stimulates the brain's neuroplasticity.
activities.actionItem: Walk or drive a different path than usual to go to a common place.

relationships.title: Digital Boundaries
relationships.description: Our constant availability can be exhausting.
relationships.actionItem: Inform that you will not respond to work or commitment messages after a certain time.

thoughts.title: The "Shoulds"
thoughts.description: Rigid demands ("I should do...", "I should be...") generate guilt.
thoughts.actionItem: Change a "Must" to an "I would like to" or "I choose to do it".

activities.title: Mood Music
activities.description: Music has the power to change our brain chemistry.
activities.actionItem: Create a playlist with 5 songs that always lift your mood.

relationships.title: Sincere Apology
relationships.description: Asking for forgiveness releases emotional burden and repairs bonds.
relationships.actionItem: If you have something pending, sincerely apologize for a small past mistake.

thoughts.title: Tunnel Vision
thoughts.description: When we are down, we only see the problem, ignoring solutions.
thoughts.actionItem: For a current problem, write 3 possible solutions, even if they seem difficult.

activities.title: Inspirational Reading
activities.description: Immersing ourselves in other stories gives us perspective.
activities.actionItem: Read 10 pages of that book you have pending on your nightstand.

relationships.title: Eye Contact
relationships.description: Eye contact fosters empathy and connection.
relationships.actionItem: Practice maintaining eye contact a bit longer than usual today.

thoughts.title: Thought Reading
thoughts.description: We believe we know what others think of us, and we are usually negative.
thoughts.actionItem: Write a thought about what you think someone thinks of you and look for an alternative explanation.

activities.title: Physical Self-care
activities.description: Taking care of the body is a sign of self-respect.
activities.actionItem: Dedicate extra time to your personal care routine (lotion, shaving, etc.).

relationships.title: Expressing Needs
relationships.description: Others cannot guess what we need.
relationships.actionItem: Use the script: "I feel [emotion] and I would need [request]" with someone close.

thoughts.title: Polarization
thoughts.description: We see the world in black or white, forgetting the gray nuances.
thoughts.actionItem: For a situation you evaluate as a "disaster", look for 2 elements that are not so bad.

activities.title: Tea and Pause
activities.description: The warmth of a drink comforts the nervous system.
activities.actionItem: Prepare your favorite tea or coffee and drink it slowly, doing nothing else.

relationships.title: Reconnection
relationships.description: Getting back in touch with valuable people increases well-being.
relationships.actionItem: Contact that friend you haven't spoken to in a while.

thoughts.title: Excessive Generalization
thoughts.description: A specific failure doesn't mean you "always" fail.
thoughts.actionItem: Identify the use of words like "always", "never", "all" in your thoughts today.

activities.title: Sing or Dance
activities.description: Expressing joy bodily tricks the brain into feeling better.
activities.actionItem: Sing a song or dance to a full track in the privacy of your home.

relationships.title: Avoiding Gossip
relationships.description: Speaking ill of others generates an environment of distrust.
relationships.actionItem: Today, if a negative comment about someone arises, try to change the subject or say something neutral.

thoughts.title: Control Fallacy
thoughts.description: We cannot control everything, and we are not responsible for everything.
thoughts.actionItem: Draw two circles: one with what you can control today and another with what you can't.

activities.title: Nearby Nature
activities.description: The green of plants and the outdoors reduce anxiety.
activities.actionItem: Go to a park or garden and sit for 10 minutes simply observing.

relationships.title: Small Detail
relationships.description: Generosity without reason strengthens relationships.
relationships.actionItem: Have a detail with someone (a post-it with a message, a candy, a coffee).

thoughts.title: Midway Point
thoughts.description: Time to reflect on the changes achieved.
thoughts.actionItem: Write a short letter to your Day 1 self encouraging them to continue.

activities.title: Refreshing Nap
activities.description: A short rest resets cognitive capacity.
activities.actionItem: If you can, take a nap of maximum 20 minutes during the day.

relationships.title: Inner Dialogue
relationships.description: How you talk to yourself influences how you relate.
relationships.actionItem: Today, treat yourself with the same kindness you would treat your best friend.

thoughts.title: Magnification
thoughts.description: Sometimes we make a mountain out of a molehill.
thoughts.actionItem: Ask yourself: "Will what I'm worried about be important in a year?"

activities.title: Cooking with Love
activities.description: Feeding ourselves well is an act of self-love.
activities.actionItem: Prepare a healthy recipe that you especially like.

relationships.title: Emotional Validation
relationships.description: Validating others' emotions makes them feel heard.
relationships.actionItem: When someone tells you something, tell them: "I understand you feel that way, it's normal."

thoughts.title: Minimization
thoughts.description: Taking importance away from your achievements prevents you from feeling capable.
thoughts.actionItem: Write down a small achievement today and allow yourself to feel pride for having done it.

activities.title: Puzzles or Games
activities.description: Focusing on a playful challenge rests the mind from worries.
activities.actionItem: Do a crossword, a sudoku or play a board game for 15 minutes.

relationships.title: Open Question
relationships.description: Open questions invite sharing more deeply.
relationships.actionItem: Instead of "How are you?", ask "What has been the most interesting part of your day?".

thoughts.title: Arbitrary Inference
thoughts.description: We reach conclusions without evidence.
thoughts.actionItem: Question a conclusion you've reached today about an ambiguous event.

activities.title: Visit a Museum or Expo
activities.description: Art inspires and takes us out of our mental routine.
activities.actionItem: Look for an online or physical exhibition and spend some time contemplating.

relationships.title: Avoiding Conflict
relationships.description: Facing disagreements calmly is better than avoiding them.
relationships.actionItem: If something bothers you today, don't avoid it; express it using an "I" message.

thoughts.title: Selective Abstraction
thoughts.description: Focusing only on the negative detail of a broad situation.
thoughts.actionItem: For an event today, look for 3 positive details that have gone unnoticed.

activities.title: Aroma-therapy
activities.description: Smells connect directly with the limbic system (emotions).
activities.actionItem: Light a scented candle or use an essential oil that relaxes you.

relationships.title: Recognize Effort
relationships.description: Thanking others' effort improves the relational climate.
relationships.actionItem: Thank someone for an effort that usually goes unnoticed.

thoughts.title: Misattribution
thoughts.description: Assigning wrong causes to your feelings.
thoughts.actionItem: If you are tired, don't think that "your life is a disaster"; think "I'm tired".

activities.title: Digital Cleanup
activities.description: Digital clutter also generates stress.
activities.actionItem: Delete unnecessary photos or files from your mobile for 10 minutes.

relationships.title: Long Hug
relationships.description: Safe physical contact releases oxytocin, the bonding hormone.
relationships.actionItem: Give a hug of more than 10 seconds to someone you trust.

thoughts.title: Perfectionism
thoughts.description: Searching for perfection is the recipe for dissatisfaction.
thoughts.actionItem: Do something today and leave it "good enough" instead of "perfect".

activities.title: Podcast or Audio-book
activities.description: Listening to new ideas helps us get out of our mental loop.
activities.actionItem: Listen to an episode of a podcast that interests you while doing something else.

relationships.title: Say What You Like
relationships.description: Often we only talk about what we don't like about others.
relationships.actionItem: Tell someone something specific you appreciate about their personality.

thoughts.title: Disqualifying the Positive
thoughts.description: "I've done it well, but it was luck". Don't take away your merit.
thoughts.actionItem: Today, when something good happens to you, say out loud: "I deserve it" or "I achieved it".

activities.title: Handicrafts
activities.description: Using hands anchors us in the present.
activities.actionItem: Do something manual: knit, fix something broken, assemble a piece of furniture.

relationships.title: Mindful Smile
relationships.description: A smile is contagious and relaxes the environment.
relationships.actionItem: Smile consciously when greeting the people you interact with today.

thoughts.title: Reward Fantasy
thoughts.description: Expecting others to change or reward us for being "good".
thoughts.actionItem: Do something good today for yourself, without expecting anyone else to notice or thank you.

activities.title: Solo Walk
activities.description: Being with oneself helps us know ourselves better.
activities.actionItem: Walk 15 minutes alone, enjoying your own company.

relationships.title: Genuine Interest
relationships.description: Curiosity for others is the basis of friendship.
relationships.actionItem: Ask someone about a hobby or topic you know they are passionate about.

thoughts.title: Social Comparison
thoughts.description: Comparing your interior with others' exterior is unfair.
thoughts.actionItem: If you compare yourself today, remember: "I only see their best version, not their complete reality."

activities.title: Free Writing
activities.description: Putting words to mental chaos orders it.
activities.actionItem: Write for 5 minutes everything that passes through your head, without filters.

relationships.title: Respecting Silence
relationships.description: Being comfortable in silence is a sign of a deep relationship.
relationships.actionItem: Don't feel forced to fill all silences in your conversations today.

thoughts.title: Confirmation Bias
thoughts.description: We only look for what confirms what we already believe.
thoughts.actionItem: Look for evidence of something positive about yourself that contradicts an old negative belief.

activities.title: Looking at Stars or Sky
activities.description: Contemplating immensity reduces the importance of our problems.
activities.actionItem: Spend a few minutes looking at the sky, clouds or stars.

relationships.title: Social Gratitude
relationships.description: Thanking the community makes us feel part of something.
relationships.actionItem: Thank the cashier, driver or waiter who serves you in a special way.

thoughts.title: Change Fallacy
thoughts.description: Expecting others to change so that we can be happy.
thoughts.actionItem: Identify something that bothers you about someone and look for how you can handle it better without the other changing.

activities.title: Caring for a Plant
activities.description: Caring for another living being fosters responsibility and calm.
activities.actionItem: Water your plants or remove dry leaves, doing it with full attention.

relationships.title: Non-Judging
relationships.description: Accepting others as they are reduces our own tension.
relationships.actionItem: Today, faced with others' behavior you usually judge, repeat to yourself: "I don't know their whole story."

thoughts.title: Being Right
thoughts.description: The obsessive need to prove we are right exhausts and alienates others.
thoughts.actionItem: In your next discussion, choose "being kind" instead of "being right".

activities.title: Therapeutic Laughter
activities.description: Laughing releases tensions immediately.
activities.actionItem: Watch a comic video or call that person who always makes you laugh.

relationships.title: Future Plans
relationships.description: Having something to look forward to together strengthens the bond.
relationships.actionItem: Propose a pleasant plan for next week to a friend or partner.

thoughts.title: Guilt
thoughts.description: Guilt anchors us in the past. Responsibility moves us to the future.
thoughts.actionItem: If you feel guilty about something, ask yourself: "What can I learn and how can I repair it?"

activities.title: Pause of "Not Doing"
activities.description: Learning to be in silence and stillness without feeling we are wasting time.
activities.actionItem: Sit for 5 minutes without mobile, without music, without doing anything, just existing.

relationships.title: Unexpected Help
relationships.description: Being useful to others gives meaning to our lives.
relationships.actionItem: Help someone today in something they haven't asked you for but you know they need.

thoughts.title: Wise Mind
thoughts.description: The balance between your rational mind and your emotional mind.
thoughts.actionItem: Before a decision today, ask yourself: "What does my logic say? What does my heart say? What does my wisdom say?"

activities.title: Review of Achievements
activities.description: Celebrating the path traveled reinforces the habit.
activities.actionItem: Dedicate 10 minutes to re-read your notes from these 29 days.

relationships.title: Extensive Gratitude
relationships.description: Thanking those around us for their presence in our lives.
relationships.actionItem: Tell your closest people how much you value having shared this month with them.

thoughts.title: Eternal Commitment
thoughts.description: Well-being is a path, not a goal.
thoughts.actionItem: Write 3 habits you've practiced this month and want to maintain forever.

activities.title: Celebration of Success
activities.description: You have completed 30 days of mindful care. Congratulations!
activities.actionItem: Do something special today to celebrate your consistency and your progress.

relationships.title: Celebrate Together
relationships.description: Sharing achievements makes them more real and lasting.
relationships.actionItem: Share your joy for having finished the challenge with someone who appreciates you.

content.prompt: Write down 3 negative thoughts you had today.
content.guide: Automatic thoughts are often quick and go unnoticed. Stop and observe them!

content.script: "Hi [Name], I wanted to thank you for [reason]. It feel great to count on you."
content.advice: Don't expect a response, the goal is to give.

content.prompt: Look for 2 pieces of evidence for and 2 against a negative thought.
content.guide: Act as an impartial judge with your own mind.

content.script: "I understand what you're saying. So, from your point of view, what happened was [summary]? I hadn't realized that."
content.advice: Paraphrasing validates the other person and shows you're really listening.

content.prompt: Write down 3 good things from today that your mind is ignoring.
content.guide: The mental filter only lets the gray through. Clean your glasses!

steps: Observe the urge and let it pass.

content.script: "Thanks for thinking of me, but I can't commit right now."
content.advice: Saying no to others is saying yes to yourself.

content.prompt: Write the worst, best, and most likely scenario.
content.guide: We tend to jump into the abyss without looking at the safe path.

content.script: "Would you give me a hand with [topic]? Your help would be great."
content.advice: Asking for help is an act of connection.

content.prompt: Describe a failure without using negative adjectives about yourself.
content.guide: You are not your mistakes, you make mistakes.

content.script: "I really admire how you've [action]. You have a lot of talent for this."
content.advice: Be specific to make it credible.

content.prompt: What logical facts contradict how you feel now?
content.guide: Feeling is not reasoning. Emotions are clouds, not the mountain.

content.script: "Tell me more about that, I'm really interested in your perspective on [topic]."
content.advice: Asking open questions invites deeper connection.

content.prompt: Which distortion has been your "star" this week?
content.guide: Knowing your pattern is the first step to breaking it.

content.description: Inhale, hold, and exhale in times of 4 seconds.

content.prompt: Map the 3 people who take care of you the most.
content.guide: Feeling supported reduces cortisol immediately.

content.prompt: Write 3 external reasons for someone's bad mood.
content.guide: Not everything is about you. Others have their battles too.

content.script: "I will be disconnected from [time]. Let's talk tomorrow."
content.advice: Your availability is not others' right.

content.prompt: Change your obligations to desires.
content.guide: Rigidity breaks, flexibility heals.

content.script: "I'm sorry, I was wrong in [action]. Thanks for understanding."
content.advice: Apologizing clears the air between two people.

content.prompt: For a problem, look for 3 "crazy" solutions.
content.guide: Broadening your view gives you freedom of choice.

content.script: "It's been a while since we talked like this, I really like being able to share this moment with you."
content.advice: Eye contact plus a warm phrase multiplies the feeling of security.

content.prompt: What else could that person be thinking?
content.guide: We are not diviners, we only project our fears.

content.script: "I feel [emotion] and it would help me if [action]."
content.advice: Being clear is being kind to others.

content.prompt: Find 2 gray nuances in a conflict.
content.guide: Life is not black or white, it's a complex rainbow.

content.script: "Hi! I remembered you a lot. Shall we talk for a bit?"
content.advice: Affection doesn't need excuses to manifest.

content.prompt: Change an "always" to a "sometimes".
content.guide: Your words design your emotional world. Choose well.

content.script: "Well, I prefer not to talk about that if they're not here. By the way, did you see what [Name] achieved yesterday? Impressive."
content.advice: Changing the subject to a positive achievement is the most elegant way to avoid negativity.

content.script: A post-it: "You are great, thanks for being here!"
content.advice: The small is big in the heart.

content.prompt: 3 lessons from your first 15 days.
content.guide: You are more capable than you were two weeks ago.

content.prompt: What would you say to someone you love in your situation?
content.guide: You also deserve your own love.

content.prompt: Will this be important in 1 year?
content.guide: Urgency of now clouds real perspective.

content.script: "I understand you feel that way, it's very normal."
content.advice: Listening is the best gift you can give.

content.prompt: Write down a success today and feel it as yours.
content.guide: It's not luck, it's your daily effort.

content.script: "What has been the best of your day today?"
content.advice: Look for the story behind the person.

content.prompt: What real evidence does your fear have?
content.guide: Your mind lies sometimes. Ask for proof.

content.script: "I'd like to chat about [topic]. I feel that..."
content.advice: Peace comes from communication, not silence.

content.prompt: Look for 3 good details hidden in the bad.
content.guide: Where you put attention, you put your life.

content.script: "I really value the effort you put into [this]."
content.advice: Thanking the process is more powerful than the result.

content.prompt: Is it the problem or is it your tiredness?
content.guide: Make sure you've slept and eaten before judging your life.

content.script: "I've had a bit of an intense day, can you give me one of those long hugs? I really need it now."
content.advice: Being vulnerable with safe people strengthens the trust bond.

content.prompt: Leave something at 80% and feel free.
content.guide: Perfect is the enemy of what makes you happy.

content.script: "I love your way of [quality]. It's inspiring."
content.advice: Don't keep the nice words, release them.

content.prompt: Say "It was me" to an achievement today.
content.guide: You are the architect of your well-being.

content.script: "Good morning! Great to see you today. How's everything going?"
content.advice: A smile and a kind phrase at the start of the day change the chemistry of the environment.

content.prompt: Do something good for you that nobody knows.
content.guide: Your own approval is the only one you need.

content.script: "What are you most passionate about in [hobby]?"
content.advice: Showing interest is loving the other.

content.prompt: Find your improvement from Day 1 to today.
content.guide: Your rival is your shadow from yesterday, no one else.

content.script: "No need for us to talk if you don't feel like it, I just like being here with you."
content.advice: Validating silence takes off pressure and creates a space of total acceptance.

content.prompt: What went BETTER today than expected?
content.guide: Your brain looks for errors. Help it see successes.

content.script: "Thank you very much! You've given me excellent service."
content.advice: Being kind is free and it changes everything.

content.prompt: How can you flow with this change today?
content.guide: Rigidity makes you unhappy, fluidity makes you free.

content.script: "It's a difficult situation, I understand you're doing what you think is best. I'm here if you need to talk."
content.advice: Accepting the other without trying to correct them is the greatest act of love.

content.prompt: Say something tender to yourself today.
content.guide: You have a home inside you, take care of it.

content.script: "Shall we do something fun next Saturday?"
content.advice: Promises of leisure feed the soul.

content.prompt: How has your internal voice changed?
content.guide: You are the result of 28 days of self-love.

content.script: "I saw you had [task] pending and I took the liberty of getting it started for you. Hope it helps!"
content.advice: Unexpected help generates a feeling of reciprocal gratitude.

content.prompt: Look for the balance between logic and emotion.
content.guide: Wisdom is the embrace of your two halves.

content.script: "Thanks for sharing this month of well-being with me."
content.advice: Celebrating together is more valuable.

content.prompt: What habit do you take with you forever?
content.guide: The challenge ends today, but your new life begins.

content.script: "We did it! I toast to us."
content.advice: Inspiring others is your new mission.

privacy.title: Privacy Protocol
privacy.intro_desc: At Mindful30, we collect the following information:
privacy.intro_item_1_desc: We use anonymous identifiers to manage your access.
privacy.intro_item_2_desc: We only sync your "Premium" status and your challenge start date to guarantee your access.
privacy.intro_item_3_desc: Your personal reflections, exercise answers, and journal are stored exclusively on your device. We do NOT have access to this content.
privacy.usage_desc: We use the collected information to:
privacy.usage_item_3: Ensure you maintain access if you change devices (account status only)
privacy.usage_note: Important note: By not syncing your reflections to the cloud for privacy, if you uninstall the application or clear your browser data, your personal writings will be lost. We recommend making manual backups if you wish.
privacy.protection_desc: We take the security of your data very seriously:
privacy.protection_item_1: We use Firebase Authentication for secure account management
privacy.protection_item_3: Data is stored on secure Google Cloud servers
privacy.protection_item_4: We implement technical and organizational security measures
privacy.protection_item_5: We do not share your personal data with third parties without your consent
privacy.rights_desc: According to the GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation), you have the right to:
privacy.rights_item_1_desc: Request a copy of your personal data
privacy.rights_item_3_desc: Request the deletion of your data
privacy.rights_item_4_desc: Receive your data in a structured format
privacy.rights_item_5_desc: Object to the processing of your data
privacy.rights_contact: To exercise any of these rights, contact us at
privacy.cookies_desc: We use cookies and similar technologies to keep your session active and improve your experience. You can configure your browser to reject cookies, although this may affect the application's functionality.
privacy.retention_desc: We retain your personal data while your account is active or as necessary to provide you with our services. If you request the deletion of your account, we will delete your personal data unless we are legally required to keep it.
privacy.changes_desc: We may update this privacy policy occasionally. We will notify you of meaningful changes by publishing the new policy on this page and updating the "last updated" date.
privacy.contact_desc: If you have questions about this privacy policy or how we handle your data, you can contact us at:

terms.title: Terms and Conditions
terms.acceptance_desc: By accessing and using Mindful30 ("the Application"), you agree to be bound by these Terms and Conditions. If you do not agree with any part of these terms, you must not use the Application.
terms.service_desc_intro: Mindful30 is a 30-day digital mental well-being program based on Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) techniques.
terms.disclaimer_important: IMPORTANT: Mindful30 is a support and mental well-being tool, NOT a substitute for professional medical or psychological treatment.
terms.disclaimer_list_1: We do not provide medical diagnoses or clinical treatment
terms.disclaimer_list_2: If you experience serious mental health symptoms, seek immediate professional help
terms.disclaimer_list_3: In case of emergency, contact local emergency services
terms.disclaimer_list_4: Always consult with a health professional before making important decisions about your well-being
terms.payment_intro: Mindful30 operates under a one-time payment model:
terms.payment_item_2_desc: There are no recurring charges or automatic renewals
terms.payment_item_3_desc: Payments are processed through Stripe, a PCI-DSS certified payment provider
terms.payment_item_4_desc: Once payment is completed, you will receive full access to the program
terms.refund_item_1: You can request a full refund within the first 14 days of purchase
terms.refund_item_2: To request a refund, send an email to info@retomindful30.online
terms.refund_item_3: Refunds will be processed within 5-10 business days
terms.refund_item_4: After 14 days, refund requests will not be accepted

terms.ip_item_2: Access is for personal, non-commercial use only
terms.ip_item_3: You may not share your account with third parties
terms.usage_intro: By using Mindful30, you commit to:
terms.usage_item_2: Maintaining the confidentiality of your account
terms.usage_item_3: Not using the application for illegal or unauthorized purposes
terms.usage_item_4: Not attempting to access restricted areas of the system

terms.modifications_desc: We reserve the right to modify or discontinue, temporarily or permanently, the service (or any part thereof) with or without prior notice. We will not be liable to you or any third party for any modification, suspension, or interruption of the service.
terms.liability_desc: To the maximum extent permitted by law, Mindful30 shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, nor for any loss of profits or income, whether directly or indirectly, nor for any loss of data, use, goodwill, or other intangible losses resulting from:
terms.liability_item_1: Your access to or use of (or inability to access or use) the service
terms.liability_item_2: Any conduct or content of third parties on the service
terms.liability_item_3: Any content obtained from the service
terms.liability_item_4: Unauthorized access, use, or alteration of your transmissions or content
terms.law_desc: These Terms shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of Spain, without giving effect to any principles of conflicts of laws.
